events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the assessment directly to `/app/answer.json`. The scenario says the field method is compliant with product instructions and project requirements, with no amendment or drawing note forbidding it, so this points to a pass with low residual risk.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"pass\",
  \"risk\": \"low\",
  \"work_stage\": \"in-progress\",
  \"component_conditions\": [],
  \"percent_complete\": 60,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.88,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Continue the installation using the documented field method and verify final conductor ampacity, temperature rating, and termination compatibility before inspection.\",
    \"Record the applicable product instruction reference and NEC 310/Table 310.16 basis in the service documentation.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed conductor selection and sizing method is less tidy than the preferred shop approach but still conforms to the listed product instructions and project requirement, and no local amendment or drawing note prohibits it. With NEC 310 and Table 310.16 available to verify ampacity and conductor sizing, the in-progress work is acceptable pending normal final checks.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NEC Article 310\",
    \"NEC Table 310.16\",
    \"Listed product installation instructions\",
    \"Project requirements\"
  ]
}
EOF
python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json >/dev/null"
